articles.summary:

articles.title: Tessera AI model offers accessible way to view Earth
articles.summary: A foundation model trained on Earth observation data from Copernicus Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 has been made widely available to researchers, it was announced at a computer industry conference this week in Denver, US.

articles.summary:
The Exploration Company (TEC), developing the reusable Nyx spacecraft, has taken a significant step forward…

articles.title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
articles.summary:
The Exploration Company has completed a key milestone in the development of its Nyx spacecraft after successfully conducting a drop test designed to validate the performance of its recovery system. Nyx is designed as a reusable space capsule that will be used to transport cargo and, potentially, crew to low Earth orbit. The company is […]
The post The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: NASA working to streamline development of nuclear electric propulsion demo mission
articles.summary:
NASA is working on a streamlined management approach for a nuclear electric propulsion demonstration mission the agency wants to launch in two and a half years.
The post NASA working to streamline development of nuclear electric propulsion demo mission appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: China builds institutional framework for space computing push
articles.summary:
China is establishing an industrial policy framework to support a push to build space-based computing infrastructure, with the emergence of influential coordinating bodies.
The post China builds institutional framework for space computing push appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: A new chapter for ESA’s brand
articles.summary: Last year, the European Space Agency (ESA) unveiled its long-term vision for the decades ahead with ESA Strategy 2040. Framed around five encompassing goals which demonstrate the important role space can play in every aspect of citizens’ lives across Europe. Today, ESA is taking a decisive step forward to bring this vision to life through a comprehensive brand transformation.

articles.title: ExoMars rover targets vast bed of clay in search for life
articles.summary: In the region where the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover will search for signs of life, clay deposits extend beyond previous estimates, a new study finds. One hypothesis even suggests a vast ocean once covered the landing site.

articles.title: A Moonlit Earth as Seen From Artemis II
articles.summary: An astronaut’s photo, taken en route to the Moon, reveals our planet and its place in space in a novel way.
